desc: APIClient.fetch returns an error dictionary on request, HTTP, or JSON-decoding failure, but process_all treats it as a list of dictionaries and crashes when calling .get on its string keys.
HTTP requests have no timeout, so an unresponsive server can cause the program to hang indefinitely.
Catching every Exception and converting it to a return value obscures programming errors and loses traceback and exception-type information.
Constructing URLs with string concatenation can produce malformed URLs when base_url or endpoint slash conventions differ.
The module-level Session and cache introduce shared mutable state, making concurrent use, test isolation, configuration, and resource lifecycle management unsafe or difficult.
get_users, get_posts, and get_todos duplicate identical fetch-and-cache logic, increasing the likelihood of inconsistent changes as more resources are added.
